name: Sauteed Shell Steak with Vinegar Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the tenderloin into eight pieces. Sprinkle with paprika on both sides.
2. Heat the butter in a skillet until very hot and almost brown; add the steaks in one layer. Cook for about 2 minutes on one side, then turn and cook for about 2 minutes or until golden brown on the other side. Remove the steaks from the skillet and keep warm.
3. Pour off the fat from the skillet. Add the vinegar, stirring to dissolve the brown particles that cling to the bottom of the skillet. Cook until the vinegar has almost evaporated.
4. Add the heavy cream. Cook, stirring over high heat until reduced almost by half. Add any juices that have accumulated around the meat.
5. Return the steaks to the skillet and turn them in the sauce.
6. Add the shallots and heat briefly.
This dish: Bifteck au vinaigre
